title: Roasted Ethiopian Yellow Pepper Stew
description: A flavorful and spicy Ethiopian-inspired stew made with roasted yellow peppers, served over injera bread.

ingredients.name: yellow peppers
ingredients.notes: any color bell pepper works but yellow is traditional

ingredients.name: roasted garlic
ingredients.notes: mince roasted garlic for added flavor

ingredients.name: onion
ingredients.notes: chop onion and sauté until softened

ingredients.name: tenderly cooked vegetables
ingredients.amount: any combination of carrots, zucchini, and cabbage
ingredients.notes: add your choice of vegetables for added nutrition

ingredients.name: olive oil
ingredients.notes: use high-quality olive oil for best flavor

ingredients.name: chili powder
ingredients.notes: adjust to taste for desired level of heat

ingredients.name: salt
ingredients.notes: season to taste with salt and pepper

instructions: Roast yellow peppers in the oven for 30-40 minutes, or until skin is blistered and charred.
Peel roasted peppers and chop into large pieces.
In a large pan, heat olive oil over medium heat. Add onion and sauté until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add minced roasted garlic, grated ginger, and chili powder. Stir to combine.
Add tenderly cooked vegetables and roasted peppers to the pan. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve over injera bread and enjoy!
Note: Traditionally, Ethiopian stews are made with a combination of vegetables and meat, but this vegetarian version is a great alternative.
